entities.name: 智能体 (agent)
entities.how_described: 本集大量讨论了智能体生成代码（agent coders）的现象，警告说如果不经过正确的架构设计，直接让智能体吐代码，得到的是一次性、极其脆弱的“快软件”，会积累庞大的技术债务。

evidence.quote: But when you think about it and you have to maintain it, you can have an agent make code for you and it could work and it could test, but is it secure?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So you've got to really understand how you're using these tools, and a lot of these things that these AI coders can do, agent coders can do, can make you incredible prototypes, do more prototypes, do more of those things to help you get that informed gut to say, "We're going this direction," architect that in and then work on the sub-segments below it and all the expert systems, expert things that you need in each of those domains.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 逆向工作 (Working Backwards)
entities.how_described: 本集认为这种“先写新闻稿”的做法其实是产品构建的基本法则，就像拍电影先写剧本一样理所应当，而不应被视为“倒着来”，因为客户只能通过营销的镜头来理解你的产品。

evidence.quote: Amazon's also very famous for that, this whole book on Working Backwards that goes really deep on this approach.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: But the weird thing is it's working backwards.

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: It's not working backwards.

evidence.quote: I feel like this is a really interesting lens into the press release first or working backwards idea, is create the infomercial first and just go extreme.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 臭鼬工厂 (skunk works)
entities.how_described: 本集将其描述为一种“先斩后奏”的创新模式：团队为了推翻领导者（如乔布斯）的固执，背着高管偷偷在地下开发诸如 iPod 支持 Windows、iPhone 加上手写笔等功能。

evidence.quote: So then it was a long story about how we finally got Windows connectivity, but there was always this skunk works thing project behind the scenes doing that.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But then when we added the stylus, which was another skunk works project, all of a sudden it came out and it was like, "Well, we had to have stylus."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So sometimes you have to have those skunk works things that even if the opinion- based leader doesn't like it, you're like, "That seems like the right thing."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 技术债务 (technical debt)
entities.how_described: 本集指出，如果过度依赖 AI 写代码而不做适当的架构和分层，得到的软件会像 H&M 的快时尚一样便宜且一次性，这种短期的收益换来的是“非常、非常长期的代价”，也就是庞大的技术债务。

evidence.quote: And that's called software debt, right, technical debt.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And everybody hates technical debt.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So it might be fixing something, but it most likely is giving more technical debt, especially when you're at the high level.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Maybe it can, but I don't think it can be if you're really going to do this because you get just technical debt and you got to start over again.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 人在回路中 (humans in the loop)
entities.how_described: 本集强烈主张，在编写代码和交付产品时，依然需要人来审查架构、安全性并决定回滚等操作，不能进行“认知投降”而把一切都交给机器。

evidence.quote: You still need humans in the loop.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: There's so many other aspects to writing code and delivering a product thing that you still need humans in the loop.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 多巴胺 (dopamine)
entities.how_described: 本集在讨论产品伦理时提到，产品经理和设计师绝不能为了数据去故意刺激用户的多巴胺、让用户上瘾，这正在撕裂社会的结构。

evidence.quote: Obviously, there's going to be innovation, and there's going to be change, but when you're really doing it, and you can see that you're doing it, and you're trying to get people hooked, or you're like, "Oh, I give more dopamine or what have you."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 快时尚 (fast fashion)
entities.how_described: 本集用其作为比喻：直接让 AI 生成的代码就像 H&M 的快时尚，便宜且是一次性的（快软件），如果想要建立真正的公司，软件就不能是这种消耗品。

evidence.quote: So like it's called fast fashion.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 提示词 (prompt)
entities.how_described: 本集提到如今很多人觉得在 AI 时代只要“写个提示词，东西就吐出来了”，嘉宾认为这种依赖是极度危险的。

evidence.quote: Today in the AI world, I can just make a prompt, and all of a sudden, it gets spit out.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And what we're saying is, "Oh, I can just, today in the AI world, I can just make a prompt and all of a sudden it gets spit out."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 网景 (Netscape)
entities.how_described: 本集将早期的 OpenAI 比作伟大的第一个网景：大家觉得它是个好用的演示工具去上了网，但新鲜感一过就会疑惑“我每天用它做什么”，然后它就蒸发了。

evidence.quote: And so it's like, "Oh yeah, you're the great first Netscape.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Everyone went out and bought Netscape Explorer or whatever it was called, Netscape Navigator.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: BlackBerry
entities.how_described: 本集提到当时只有 1% 到 2% 的手机用户拥有它，苹果内部曾为是否要去讨好这群极客用户而陷入争论，最终决定放弃物理键盘，转而瞄准剩下 98% 的大众市场。

evidence.quote: I want to start with the BlackBerry.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I was just watching the Blackberry movie recently, and it's kind of this journey of the BlackBerry founders and their story.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: There was one way of looking at the BlackBerry, which was that is the market we want to go after or we want to win.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And then there's the other side, the flip side of that argument, which is only 1% or 2% of mobile phone users at the time had a BlackBerry, knew what a BlackBerry was.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So it was all of those things just on the verge to say, this is what's going to be very different than what just came before it, like a Blackberry.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Humane
entities.how_described: 本集指出该公司试图在手掌上做小投影来取代屏幕的设备路线，是“不同，但并没有更好”。

evidence.quote: We saw what happened with Humane, all that other stuff.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: That is so interesting. And you're not a fan of, like, the Humane approach; I think it was like a little projection on your hand as a screen...
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: ... have the humane approach, I think, was a little projection on your hand as a screen replacement.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: ChatGPT
entities.how_described: 本集提到消费者会花钱试用它，但发现每月支付 20 美元后，得到的只是一个类似“Siri 1.0”的体验，从而对它失去耐心。

evidence.quote: Today people are like, "Yeah, I'm going to try ChatGPT or whatever.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0
